one_line_takeaway: LOGICPOISON shows that GraphRAG's structural defenses against content-injection attacks can be bypassed entirely by swapping existing same-type entities to rewire the graph's logical topology, achieving higher attack success than PoisonedRAG at zero injected tokens and near-random (AUC 0.57) perplexity-based detectability.
key_finding: LOGICPOISON outperforms PoisonedRAG across all tested dataset/LLM/RAG-architecture combinations in Table 1. Representative results (ASR / ASR-G, %): on HotpotQA with GPT-4o-mini, GraphRAG: PoisonedRAG 66.8/80.0 vs. LogicPoison 78.4/92.2; GFM-RAG: PoisonedRAG 71.6/70.4 vs. LogicPoison 81.0/78.0. On MuSiQue with GPT-4o-mini, GraphRAG: PoisonedRAG 59.6/77.6 vs. LogicPoison 91.4/97.0 -- the largest margin, attributed to MuSiQue's deeper multi-hop reasoning demands. Against non-graph-aware attacks on 2Wiki (Table 2), LogicPoison (e.g., 78.4/95.6 ASR/ASR-G on GraphRAG with GPT-4o-mini) substantially beats Prompt Injection (58.8/80.4), Disinformation (54.6/75.0), Naive Attack (18.8/15.6), and GCG Attack (19.6/21.6). Efficiency (Table 3, averaged over 3 datasets): LOGICPOISON's full pipeline takes 1406.4s vs. PoisonedRAG's 6607.3s (~4.7x faster, paper states '4 times faster'); token cost 74.9 vs. 593.6 (~1/8, matching the paper's claim); and injected tokens = 0 vs. 296,813 for PoisonedRAG, since LogicPoison only substitutes existing entities rather than inserting new text. Defense results: query paraphrasing reduces ASR by less than 1.0 percentage point across all dataset/system combinations (Table 4, e.g., HotpotQA/GraphRAG 92.2->92.2, delta 0). Perplexity-based detection achieves AUC=0.57 (near-random, 0.5 baseline), meaning the poisoned corpus is nearly indistinguishable from clean text by PPL. HippoRAG2 shows comparatively greater resilience than Microsoft GraphRAG and GFM-RAG (smaller ASR gap versus PoisonedRAG), and GFM-RAG is noted as more defensively robust than Microsoft GraphRAG generally, attributed to its GNN-based denoising of the KG as an intermediate reasoning state.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is LOGICPOISON, a poisoning attack against GraphRAG (Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems that corrupts the LOGICAL TOPOLOGY of the constructed knowledge graph rather than injecting false or LLM-generated content into the corpus. The method has three stages: (1) Strategic Entity Selection combining a 'Global Logic Poison' pool (entities selected by corpus-level document frequency f(e,τ) as a proxy for topological hub importance, top-n% per entity type, n%=5% in experiments) and a 'Query-Centric Logic Poison' pool (entities extracted via LLM Chain-of-Thought reasoning per multi-hop query as bridge/comparison entities, then verified against the corpus); (2) Type-Preserving Entity Swapping, which applies a deterministic cyclic permutation π_τ over entities of the same NER type (e.g., swapping PERSON entities like Obama/Trump/Biden with each other) so replacements remain grammatically and semantically plausible; (3) corpus rewriting, where every mention of a targeted entity is substituted with its permuted counterpart, without modifying queries or ground-truth answers, so the poisoned corpus 'reroutes' valid reasoning chains (A→B→C) into logical dead ends or spurious paths (A→B'→C) while the graph indexing pipeline (chunking, embedding, entity/relation extraction) is left untouched -- no malicious text is injected at all.
threat_model: Black-box attacker with corpus-poisoning-level access (can modify/insert documents into the source corpus before the GraphRAG system builds its index) but no knowledge of the target system's internal graph construction algorithm or its specific implementation (Microsoft GraphRAG, HippoRAG2, GFM-RAG, or Naive RAG are all treated as opaque). The attacker also has query-side knowledge (the set of queries Q to be answered) which it uses via an auxiliary LLM to extract candidate bridge entities via Chain-of-Thought, but does not need white-box access to the target LLM or the retrieval/reasoning engine. The attack goal is to make the system produce an incorrect answer (ASR: ground-truth substring absent from output) while remaining stealthy/imperceptible at the text level (grammatically coherent, low injected-token footprint, and low perplexity so it evades both community-summarization/filtering defenses and PPL-based poisoned-text detectors).
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) experiments are confined to static-corpus graph indices; robustness/persistence of the attack under dynamic graph updating (nodes added/pruned in real time) is untested; (2) evaluation is English-only -- extending imperceptible entity swapping to morphologically rich/inflected languages is left to future work. Additional limitations evident from the methodology itself (not explicitly flagged by authors): the Query-Centric pool construction assumes attacker access to (or knowledge of) the target query set Q at poisoning time, which is a nontrivial assumption in some deployment settings; the entity-frequency heuristic for 'global hub' importance is only a proxy for true graph centrality since the graph does not exist prior to construction, and the paper's own theoretical justification (Appendix, scale-free/percolation arguments) is asserted rather than empirically validated against ground-truth centrality metrics on the constructed graphs; only two of the three GraphRAG systems (Microsoft GraphRAG and GFM-RAG) are used in the defense/ablation studies (Table 4, Figure 3), with HippoRAG2 excluded from some analyses.

quantitative_results: HotpotQA/GPT-4o-mini/GraphRAG: LogicPoison ASR 78.4 / ASR-G 92.2 vs PoisonedRAG ASR 66.8 / ASR-G 80.0
MuSiQue/GPT-4o-mini/GraphRAG: LogicPoison ASR 91.4 / ASR-G 97.0 vs PoisonedRAG ASR 59.6 / ASR-G 77.6
2Wiki/GPT-4o-mini/GraphRAG vs traditional attacks: LogicPoison 78.4/95.6 vs Prompt Injection 58.8/80.4, Disinformation 54.6/75.0, GCG Attack 19.6/21.6, Naive Attack 18.8/15.6
Efficiency (averaged over 3 datasets): LOGICPOISON time 1406.4s / token cost 74.9 / injected tokens 0, vs PoisonedRAG time 6607.3s / token cost 593.6 / injected tokens 296813
Query Paraphrasing defense: ASR drop always <1.0 percentage point (e.g., HotpotQA/GraphRAG 92.2 -> 92.2, delta 0.0; MuSiQue/GraphRAG 97.0 -> 96.2, delta -0.8)
